    • AppointmentController

      public AppointmentController()

  • Method Details

    • reportsButton

      public void reportsButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException
      This method navigates the user to the reports screen.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The event is triggered when the user clicks the Reports button.
      Throws:
      IOException - is thrown if the ReportsScreen file can't be loaded.

    • viewCustomersButton

      public void viewCustomersButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException
      This method navigates the user to the customer information screen.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The event is triggered when the user clicks the Customer Information Screen button.
      Throws:
      IOException - is thrown if the CustomerInformationScreen file can't be loaded.

    • allAppointmentsOnAction

      public void allAppointmentsOnAction(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      This method fetches all appointments from the database. Populates the appointments table with the retrieved data.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - is triggered when the user selects the option to view all appointments.

    • weekAppointmentsOnAction

      public void weekAppointmentsOnAction(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Displays the current week's appointments in the appointments table view. This method fetches the current week's appointments.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - is triggered when the user selects the Weekly Appointments radio button.

    • monthsAppointmentsOnAction

      public void monthsAppointmentsOnAction(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      Displays the current month's appointments in the appointments table view. This method fetches the current month's appointments.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - is triggered when the user selects the Monthly Appointments radio button.

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL url, ResourceBundle resourceBundle)
      This method initializes the controller class. This method is called automatically after the fxml file has been loaded. It populates the appointments table in the AppointmentScreen.fxml.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable
      Parameters:
      url - The location used to resolve relative paths for the root object, or null if the location is not known.
      resourceBundle - The resource bundle to use for localizing the root object or null if the root object was not localized.

    • addAppointmentButton

      public void addAppointmentButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException
      This method handles the add appointment button event. It takes you to the AddAppointmentScreen and allows you to add a new appointment to the Appointments table.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The add appointment button click event.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs while loading the AddAppointmentScreen

    • modifyAppointmentButton

      public void modifyAppointmentButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws IOException
      This method handles the modify appointment button for the appointments table. It will take you to a new screen that allows you to modify an appointment. If no appointment is selected an alert will prompt you to first select an appointment to modify.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The modify appointment button click event.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an error occurs while loading the ModifyAppointmentScreen

    • deleteAppointmentButton

      public void deleteAppointmentButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent) throws SQLException
      This method handles the delete appointment button. It allows you to delete a selected appointment from the appointments table. A pop-up will ask if your sure you want to delete. If you click OK, the appointment will be deleted. If you click Cancel, the appointment will not be deleted and no changes will be made.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The delete appointment button click event.
      Throws:
      SQLException

    • signOutButton

      public void signOutButton(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      This method handles the Sign-Out button When the Sign-Out button is clicked, the user is signed out and exits the application.
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - The Sigh-Out button click event.
